name: How do I practically track whether my local voice search efforts are working without fancy tools?
description: Use what you already have: Google Business Profile insights for calls and directions, Search Console for conversational and “near me” queries, and simple notes

mainEntity.name: How do I practically track whether my local voice search efforts are working without fancy tools?

acceptedAnswer.text:
To measure local voice search performance, you’ll combine several indirect metrics rather than a single “voice” report. Track changes in calls, direction requests, map views, and question-based queries over time.

These behaviors typically rise when assistants start recommending your business more often.

KEY FACTORS
- Use Google Business Profile insights for calls, messages, and direction taps.
- Check Search Console for longer, conversational queries and “near me” phrases.
- Look at referral data from maps and assistant apps where available.

Create a simple monthly dashboard to compare these numbers before and after voice-focused changes like FAQ additions or listing cleanups.

You’re looking for more high-intent actions, not just keyword rankings, as your primary success signal.
acceptedAnswer.description: Use what you already have: Google Business Profile insights for calls and directions, Search Console for conversational and “near me” queries, and simple notes on customer feedback (“I asked my phone”). Rising high-intent actions are your best indicator that voice optimization is paying off.
